old_claim.note: Basic Hunting & Gathering is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: Band organization is the small-scale political/social form associated with gatherer-hunter subsistence, so hunting and gathering is contextual economic infrastructure rather than a separate earlier predecessor.

source_shape.source_locator: Section 8.2 states that band societies are gatherer-hunters living in small groups of 20 to 30 people, strongly egalitarian, cooperative, sharing-oriented, and making decisions through discussion and consensus.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: OpenStax presents band societies as the small-group social form of gatherer-hunters.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports hunting and gathering as the economic context for the social organization, while retyping away from an inaccurate predecessor relationship.

ontology_before: The edge treated Basic Hunting & Gathering as an earlier predecessor of Tribalism, implying a staged sequence from subsistence to a broader tribal category.
ontology_after: The node is now scoped to band-level gatherer-hunter organization, with hunting and gathering modeled as contextual economic infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hunting_gathering_basic remains connected but is retyped from historical_predecessor to common_dependency.
would_reject_if: The node were split so that a separate later tribal-society node, not band organization, inherited this edge.
A source showed the graph should model a strict staged transition from hunting and gathering to band organization.
The hunting_gathering_basic node were narrowed away from gatherer-hunter subsistence.
short_reason: Band organization is the social form of gatherer-hunter lifeways, not a later technology descended from them.
